title: Defining What It Means to Be a Digital Leader — Enterprise Corporate Strategy
situation: Manulife had committed publicly to becoming a digital leader, but enterprise-wide there was no shared definition of what 'digital leadership' actually meant or how the organization would measure progress against it. Executive priorities, investment decisions, and KPIs across regions and business lines were not anchored to a common framework, so 'being digital' meant different things to different leaders.
obstacle: Translating an aspirational strategic narrative into measurable corporate strategy required reconciling competing definitions across regions, business segments, and functions. Each area had its own digital initiatives and preferred metrics. Building a single framework that would influence enterprise-wide KPIs meant securing alignment from leaders who had each been telling their own digital story.
actions: Co-developed the enterprise corporate strategy framework for measuring digital leadership across Global Wealth & Asset Management
Translated the digital leadership ambition into a structured set of dimensions, capabilities, and outcome measures
Influenced the enterprise KPI suite to reflect the new framework and align measurement with strategic intent
Worked across regions and business lines to build shared understanding and adoption
Presented and defended the framework with senior leaders, integrating feedback while preserving analytical rigor
results: Framework adopted as the basis for enterprise digital leadership measurement
Enterprise KPIs reshaped to track digital progress against a defined standard
Significant influence on enterprise-wide strategic priorities and investment focus
fullStory: Manulife had committed to digital leadership as a public ambition, but inside the organization there was no agreed-upon definition. I co-developed the enterprise corporate strategy framework for measuring it. The work required translating an aspiration into measurable dimensions, then aligning regions and business lines that had each been telling their own digital story. The framework reshaped the enterprise KPI suite and gave leaders a shared language for prioritization and investment. This is the kind of work where you do not own the strategy, but you curate it, sharpen it, and turn it into something the organization can actually act on.
